name: Lester Bowie
description: American jazz trumpet player and composer (1941-1999)
abstract: Lester Bowie (October 11, 1941 – November 8, 1999) was an American jazz trumpet player and composer. He was a member of the Association for the Advancement of Creative Musicians and co-founded the Art Ensemble of Chicago.

about_quotes.speaker: Roscoe Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: the first time we went there it was with Lester Bowie and Philip Wilson and myself... all of these people in the Art Ensemble.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roscoe Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 23, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: he invited me to be a part of Brass Fantasy, and so I got to be the bass player in that group of all brass players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Stewart interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: A friend from Chicago days, Bowie played intermittently with DeJohnette until the end of his life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: Lester Bowie has a humor about him. He wasn’t all “oh boy, this is,” you know. And they could play.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: Lester Bowie was interviewed one time and he said... that’s when they started the AACM.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: Lester Bowie does Louis Armstrong. They come, I mean that skipped bebop.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022
